source.title: Dorsal anterior cingulate and ventromedial prefrontal cortex have inverse roles in both foraging and economic choice

confidence_rationale: Direct re-analysis and extension of the Kolling 2012 paradigm showing dACC activity is better accounted for by choice difficulty than by foraging value specifically. Critically disciplines the SD-032b specification by distinguishing 'hard to choose' from 'foraging-mode engaged'. Mixed direction because it supports SD-032b's general commitment/switching role but weakens the specific Kolling interpretation.
failure_signatures: A substrate that only outputs a 'foraging value signal' (EV(search) - EV(stay)) without a corresponding 'choice difficulty' signal (the sharpness of the EV distribution over modes) will fail to reproduce Shenhav 2016's finding that dACC tracks difficulty even in economic choices. SD-032b should output both.

A substrate whose switching signal saturates identically whether the decision is easy (clear winner) or hard (tied alternatives) will show a different failure signature than biological dACC, which is MAXIMAL for hard choices of moderate value -- not for obviously high-value switches.

mapping.source_claim_statement: In a re-analysis and extension of Kolling, Behrens, Mars & Rushworth (2012), Shenhav et al. show dACC activity is better accounted for by choice difficulty (how closely valued the alternatives are) than by foraging value specifically. The same inverse-to-vmPFC pattern appears in both foraging and economic choice regimes, suggesting a common function: dACC and vmPFC play complementary roles in choices of either type, with dACC reflecting difficulty/conflict and vmPFC reflecting value of the chosen option. There is not a unique foraging-mode circuit.
mapping.ree_translation: This paper critically disciplines SD-032b's output specification. The dACC-analog should emit at least two things: (1) a mode-value comparison (EVC or foraging-value) and (2) a choice-difficulty/uncertainty signal (variance or entropy of the mode-value distribution). The switching threshold (MECH-259) is most likely gated by difficulty -- the coordinator switches easily when one mode clearly dominates, struggles when modes are tied. This matters for REE: a substrate that fires mode-switch whenever z_harm_a spikes will be correct when other modes obviously dominate but pathologically trigger-happy when the organism is in a genuinely ambiguous 'should I break commitment or not' state.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The Shenhav vs Kolling debate is unresolved. Kolling lab has published responses defending the foraging-value interpretation. The most honest stance is that dACC probably reflects BOTH foraging value AND choice difficulty, and the two are correlated in many experimental paradigms. For ree-v3 the practical implication is that SD-032b should emit multiple signals (EV-of-switching AND difficulty-of-switching) and we should not commit to one interpretation.
mapping.source_context: Human fMRI re-analysis of Kolling 2012 paradigm plus extended experiments with larger samples and broader foraging-value ranges. Argues the original Kolling result is explained by choice difficulty once biases and range are corrected.

confidence_components.notes: Solid methodological work that directly engages with the Kolling interpretation. Published in a competent but less flagship venue than Kolling's original Science paper. The debate is unresolved in the field; neither side has a killer empirical test. High value for ree-v3 because it forces us to emit multiple SD-032b signals rather than one.
